_comment: Single source of truth for the governed SDK surface allowlist (AC-074 / REQ-053). Both the Python suite (src/python/tests/test_surface.py) and the TypeScript suite (src/ts/tests/surface.test.ts) load THIS file — there is no per-binding duplicate literal, so the two bindings cannot carry divergent copies (cross-binding parity, P2). Each suite asserts its INTROSPECTED live command surface is a SUBSET of `allowlist` (P1, never equality) and that `core` is present and live. As of Slice 30 the four `read.*` members are LIVE: each suite introspects the `read` namespace and asserts the four verbs are present-in-the-live-surface (a removal fails), in addition to the subset check. 0.8.6 Slice 10 (OPP-5): `embed` is a governed read-path primitive (Engine.embed, same name in Py+TS) — HITL-signed 2026-06-26; it was shipped Python-only in 0.8.4 and is now brought to Py↔TS parity. 0.8.19 Slice 10 (OPP-12 record-lifecycle Phase-1): `transition` and `purge` are net-new governed lifecycle verbs (Engine.transition/Engine.purge, same names in Py+TS) — the AC-074 governed-surface delta, HITL-SIGNED 2026-07-10 at the 0.8.19 Slice-40 close (delta also carries the non-command types `LifecycleState`/`IllegalTransitionError`/`NotLifecycleAddressableError`/`IdSpace`/`IdSpaceKind`, `PreparedWrite::Node` +`state`/+`reason`, and the `SearchHit.id` `u64→IdSpace` retype; recovery_denylist UNCHANGED — five names; Phase-2 read-view/validity/projection types excluded). Proposed in the Slice-0 design §10. ***0.8.20 Slice 5d (R-20-E4, erasure) — AWAITING HITL SIGN-OFF, NOT SIGNED.*** `erase_source`/`eraseSource` (Engine.erase_source in Python, Engine.eraseSource in TypeScript) is a net-new governed lifecycle verb: the erasure counterpart to `purge`. `purge` addresses a GOVERNED node by `logical_id`; `erase_source` addresses ANONYMOUS content (rows with no `logical_id`) by its provenance, which `purge` cannot reach — so before this delta an SDK-only consumer with no CLI on `PATH` could not discharge a deletion obligation over anonymous content at all (R-20-E4). The delta ALSO carries: the non-command report types `EraseReport` (Py `fathomdb._fathomdb.EraseReport`, TS `EraseReport`) and, on the Rust facade, the net-new public `SourceId` provenance newtype from Slice 5c (carried here per Slice-5c follow-up FU-5c-2) plus `ExciseReport` moving from the operator-gated re-export block to the always-present one (it is `erase_source`'s return type). `excise_source` REMAINS CLI-only and is deliberately NOT allowlisted — it stays the recovery seam, and it alone may address the engine's reserved `_`-prefixed provenance namespace. recovery_denylist UNCHANGED — still exactly the five REQ-054 names; `erase_source` is not one of them, so AC-041 is unaffected and was re-run green. This entry is a PROPOSAL recorded so the branch is not red; the orchestrator is escalating it for the AC-079 sign-off. ***0.8.20 Slice 10b (R-20-RV / R-20-NV, read-view + node validity) — AWAITING HITL SIGN-OFF, NOT SIGNED.*** This delta is deliberately SMALL BY CONSTRUCTION. `ReadView` is threaded as an OPTIONAL PARAMETER on the five existing read verbs (`read.get`, `read.get_many`, `read.list`, `graph.neighbors`, and `read.list` again via its `filter=` form) rather than shipped as five `*_with_view` sibling verbs: sibling verbs would have added roughly eight new allowlist members across the two binding naming conventions, whereas threading adds ZERO new verb names for the read path and leaves every existing signature source-compatible in Python and TypeScript (the parameter is optional and defaults to the strict view). The delta therefore carries exactly ONE net-new command, `read.crossed_boundary_since` / `read.crossedBoundarySince` (Engine.crossed_boundary_since in Python, read.crossedBoundarySince in TypeScript) — the R-20-NV validity-boundary hook, which has no existing verb to ride on: it answers 'which nodes entered or left validity since instant T', a question no read verb can express. The delta ALSO carries the non-command types `ReadView` and `BoundaryCrossing` (Py `fathomdb.types.ReadView` / `fathomdb.types.BoundaryCrossing`, TS `ReadView` / `BoundaryCrossing`, and both on the Rust facade). recovery_denylist UNCHANGED — still exactly the five REQ-054 names; none of `crossed_boundary_since` / `ReadView` / `BoundaryCrossing` is one of them and none collides with `rebuild`, so AC-041 is unaffected and was re-run green. `history_as_of` / transaction-time is explicitly OUT OF SCOPE and is NOT part of this delta. This entry is a PROPOSAL recorded so the branch is not red; the orchestrator is escalating it for sign-off. ***0.8.20 Slice 15b (TC-34, node-validity WRITE path) — AWAITING HITL SIGN-OFF, NOT SIGNED.*** This delta adds ZERO commands. Slice 10b shipped the node validity axis READ-ONLY (the `valid_from`/`valid_until` columns, the `ReadView` validity predicate and `read.crossed_boundary_since`), leaving a window a caller could filter on but could never set — authoring one required raw SQL against `canonical_nodes`. TC-34 closes that gap with two OPTIONAL FIELDS on the existing node write item, `PreparedWrite::Node` +`valid_from`/+`valid_until` (`validFrom`/`validUntil` and `valid_from`/`valid_until` both accepted in TypeScript, `valid_from`/`valid_until` in Python), carried on the ALREADY-GOVERNED `write` verb. Fields rather than a new top-level verb is the deliberate choice and follows the in-repo precedent exactly: `PreparedWrite::Edge` has accepted its temporal `t_valid`/`t_invalid` fields on the same batch item since Slice 30. So the allowlist MEMBERSHIP is byte-unchanged by this slice — nothing is added to `allowlist` or `core`. The values are INTEGER epoch seconds and the window is half-open [valid_from, valid_until); an omitted bound is unbounded on that side, so a write that omits both lands NULL/NULL and is behaviour-identical to every pre-slice write (asserted against the RAW TABLE, not a read verb). A `valid_from >= valid_until` pair can never match any instant and is refused with the EXISTING typed `InvalidArgument`/`InvalidArgumentError`; a non-integer bound is refused with the EXISTING `WriteValidation`/`WriteValidationError`. No new error type is minted. recovery_denylist UNCHANGED — still exactly the five REQ-054 names; neither field name collides with `recover`/`restore`/`repair`/`fix`/`rebuild`, so AC-041 is unaffected and was re-run green. Gated by requirement id + TDD test name (engine `slice15b_node_validity_write.rs`, TS `slice15b-node-validity-write.test.ts`, Py `test_slice15b_node_validity_write.py`) rather than by minting a new AC — AC-079 remains UNMINTED. This entry is a PROPOSAL recorded so the branch is not red; the orchestrator is escalating it for sign-off. ***0.8.20 Slice 15d (R-20-PR / R-20-EAV, projection registry + EAV/property-FTS) — AWAITING HITL SIGN-OFF, NOT SIGNED.*** This delta adds TWO net-new governed commands: `configure_projections`/`configureProjections` (Engine.configure_projections in Python, Engine.configureProjections in TypeScript) — the declarative, idempotent projection-registry apply (C-1: the engine is the SOLE projection authority; explicit `drop`, destructive-change refusal, graceful-deferred `rankable`/`searchable→vector`) — and `read.projections` (a single name in BOTH bindings: Python `fathomdb.read.projections`, TypeScript `read.projections` — the registry introspection folded into the `read.*` namespace, C5). The delta ALSO carries the non-command types `ProjectionSpec` / `ProjectionDelta` / `ProjectionRole` (Py `fathomdb.types.*` + native `fathomdb._fathomdb.*`, TS exports; the `ProjectionSpec.vector` sub-object is where Slice 20's `dense_readiness` attaches additively) and one net-new typed error, `ProjectionDestructiveError` (Py `fathomdb.errors.ProjectionDestructiveError` carrying `name`/`delta`, TS `ProjectionDestructiveError` mapped from the `FDB_PROJECTION_DESTRUCTIVE` envelope). recovery_denylist UNCHANGED — still exactly the five REQ-054 names; none of `configure_projections`/`configureProjections`/`read.projections` collides with `recover`/`restore`/`repair`/`fix`/`rebuild`, so AC-041 is unaffected and was re-run green. Gated by requirement id + TDD test name (engine `slice15d_projection_registry.rs`, schema `step24_migration.rs`, TS `slice15d-projection-registry.test.ts`, Py `test_slice15d_projection_registry.py`) rather than by minting a new AC — AC-079 remains UNMINTED. This entry is a PROPOSAL recorded so the branch is not red; the orchestrator is escalating it for sign-off.
